text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: react-datepicker supports a `withPortal` prop that renders the calendar into a React portal instead of inline/popper-positioned next to the input. When `withPortal` is set (and no `portalId` override is used), the component currently wraps its output in an extra, unnecessary `<div>` around the input container and the portal — an element with no styling purpose that does not exist in the equivalent non-portal render path. Concretely, rendering `<DatePicker withPortal />` produces a DOM shape where the element with class `react-datepicker__input-container` is nested one level deeper than it should be, inside a superfluous wrapping `<div>`, instead of being a direct child of whatever container the caller rendered `DatePicker` into. The fix should make the input container a direct child of the render container in the `withPortal` case too, with no extra wrapping element in between (the portaled calendar content itself still renders elsewhere via React's portal mechanism — only the unnecessary wrapper around the input container should go away).

Environment notes: no root access is needed. Dependencies are already installed via `corepack enable && yarn install --immutable`. Run tests with Jest, e.g. `node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/datepicker_test.test.tsx` to scope to the datepicker component tests. Fix the library source (likely in `src/index.tsx`, wherever the portal render branch builds its returned JSX); do not just add workarounds in test files.

text: I’ll inspect the portal render path and existing datepicker tests, then make the smallest source change and run the focused Jest suite. I’ll also check prior Nowledge context for any related implementation decisions before concluding.

input.query: react-datepicker withPortal extra wrapper input-container portal render bug

content.text: {"results":[{"id":"e4fae2ba-9738-42af-80e7-e9a191604a67","memory_id":"e4fae2ba-9738-42af-80e7-e9a191604a67","title":"react-datepicker multi-month custom header navigation reset","content":"In react-datepicker, day selection stores the visible panel index in DatePicker state.monthSelectedIn, which Calendar uses to offset the rendered months. A custom header's changeMonth callback changes Calendar's internal date but does not clear that index. The fix is to adapt renderCustomHeader in DatePicker and reset monthSelectedIn to 0 before delegating to the Calendar callback, preserving panel-origin behavior for day selection while making custom month jumps start at the leftmost panel. Regression coverage belongs in the DatePicker test suite with monthsShown={2}, a day click in the second month, and a custom-header changeMonth assertion.","relevance_score":0.829191677647939,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (50%) + Text Match (27%) + Keyword Match (23%)"},{"id":"a143c07e-456e-41c6-8438-c4cfb2028177","memory_id":"a143c07e-456e-41c6-8438-c4cfb2028177","title":"react-datepicker monthHeaderPosition implementation","content":"Implemented monthHeaderPosition as a public DatePicker/Calendar prop with top (default), middle, and bottom modes. Top preserves the existing month-container header and calendar-wide navigation; middle/bottom pass each month’s header into Month around/below its day grid, with localized previous/next buttons and month-date-based min/max disabled checks. TypeScript and focused Calendar/DatePicker Jest suites pass.","relevance_score":0.7479457367804159,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (52%) + Text Match (37%) + Keyword Match (11%)"},{"id":"4c4e93ee-9e90-475b-ac0f-014337dd95c8","memory_id":"4c4e93ee-9e90-475b-ac0f-014337dd95c8","title":"useCombobox blur selection semantics","content":"In Downshift's useCombobox hook, input blur must use the blur event's relatedTarget to distinguish deliberate focus movement from focus leaving the document. Dispatch InputBlur with selectItem: event.relatedTarget !== null (or equivalent truthiness check): a non-null relatedTarget preserves existing auto-selection of the highlighted item, while null closes the menu and clears the highlight without changing selectedItem/inputValue. Regression coverage belongs in src/hooks/useCombobox/__tests__/getInputProps.test.js.","relevance_score":0.5866065030928946,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (64%) + Text Match (36%)"},{"id":"88046863-56eb-4f1f-8d0e-7de6a05ce5af","memory_id":"88046863-56eb-4f1f-8d0e-7de6a05ce5af","title":"Downshift aria-selected props are booleans","content":"Downshift useCombobox and useSelect getItemProps now return boolean aria-selected values directly from their comparisons. The prior template-literal coercion produced truthy string values; DOM aria-selected serialization remains unchanged. Focused regression assertions and the full Jest suite pass.","relevance_score":0.5815926725268363,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (100%)"},{"id":"70599eb5-3c17-466e-84fe-1e457217a170","memory_id":"70599eb5-3c17-466e-84fe-1e457217a170","title":"Exclude adjacent-month filler cells from range preview","content":"In react-datepicker, Day.isInSelectingRange must first verify that the cell date's month matches the Month component's month prop. Calendar grids include adjacent-month filler cells, and date-range interval checks alone otherwise mark those cells with react-datepicker__day--in-selecting-range. The guard belongs in src/day.tsx so it also prevents selecting-range start/end modifiers from being applied to filler cells. Standalone Day tests must provide a realistic month prop (getMonth(day)).","relevance_score":0.5651628505250039,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (70%) + Text Match (19%) + Keyword Match (11%)"}],"total":5,"query":"react-datepicker withPortal extra wrapper input-container portal render bug","retrieval_mode":"strict","retrieval_space_ids":["default"]}

text: The defect is exactly in `DatePicker.render()`: the `withPortal` branch returns an outer `<div>` containing the input container and portal content. I’ll replace that structural wrapper with a React fragment so both pieces remain siblings without introducing a DOM node.
The source change is applied and the focused Jest run is still executing; no test failures have appeared so far. Once it completes, I’ll verify the final diff and working tree.
